final_response: Flagged and filed. I routed this to observations.md rather than decisions.md because what you described is a recurring environmental pattern (a domain rule about IT lead time), not a commitment that constrains future action. The entry — '2026-05-17 — Contractor access provisioning ~4 day lag' — names the pattern (Feb / March / May), states the rule (treat IT provisioning as ~4-business-day lead time for external joiners; kick off before nominal start date), and notes that if we ever decide to formalize a workaround (e.g. a pre-provisioning checklist tied to contract signing), that should be promoted into decisions.md with a reference back. I also opened today's daybook (2026-05-17.md) and logged the flag and the routing rationale in the Log section, with a brief Handoff. No carry-over for the next session.

actions.content_summary: New daybook for today. Intent marked reactive. Log captures the user's flag and the routing rationale (observations.md, not decisions.md). Handoff notes no carry-over and the path to promote into a decision later if a workaround is formalized. Generated-by: mixed.

notes: User's message described a pattern observed three times across months — that's the observations.md shape (a domain rule future sessions would re-derive), not decisions.md (which is for commitments that constrain future action).
Did not create a project folder — the user framed this as a flag, not a workstream. If follow-through is needed, a project can be spun up later.
Did not invent a commitment on the user's behalf (e.g. 'we'll always pre-provision N days early'). Left that as a hypothetical promotion path in both the observation and the daybook.
Used canonical heading slug format '## YYYY-MM-DD — Short title' per AGENTS.md guidance on stable anchors.
Avoided em-dashes inside the title portion of the heading (used an em-dash only as the date/title separator, per the example in AGENTS.md).
